originalTitle: Fifa to pay Somali referee Artan full World Cup fee
title: A Special Referee from Somalia Gets Paid Even Without Going to the Big Game!
content:
Our Amazing Referee, Omar!

Hello, super sports fans! Today, we have a heartwarming story from our amazing continent, Africa. It's about a very special person named Omar Artan. He is a football referee from a country called Somalia, right here in Africa! Omar is really good at his job. What does a referee do, you ask? Well, imagine a football game with two teams playing. Sometimes, players might disagree, or they might not follow the rules. That's where the referee comes in! The referee is like a judge on the football field. They make sure everyone plays fair and follows all the rules. They blow their whistle, show yellow or red cards, and keep the game fun and safe for everyone. Omar Artan is one of these important people. He is a very talented referee, and he works hard to make sure football games are fair. He wears a special uniform, usually black, and runs all over the field to watch everything. It's a tough job, but someone has to do it, and Omar does it with a big smile and lots of fairness!

The Biggest Football Party: The World Cup!

Now, let's talk about something super exciting: The World Cup! Have you heard of it? The World Cup is the biggest football party in the whole wide world! It happens only once every four years. Teams from different countries all over the globe come together to play football and find out who is the best. It's like a huge, grand celebration of football! Millions and millions of people watch the World Cup on TV, and thousands go to the stadiums to cheer for their favorite teams. It's a time when everyone, young and old, gets excited about football. For the next World Cup, many games will be played in faraway countries like the United States of America (USA), Canada, and Mexico. That's a long way from Africa! **Did you know?** The very first FIFA World Cup was held in 1930 in Uruguay, a country far away in South America! Only 13 teams played in that first tournament. Omar Artan, our talented referee from Somalia, was chosen to be part of this incredible event! Imagine that! He was picked because he is one of the best referees around. It's a huge honor to be chosen to officiate (that means to be the referee) at the World Cup. He must have been so proud, and his country, Somalia, must have been very proud of him too!

A Little Problem for Omar

So, Omar was all set to go to the United States to help referee the big World Cup games. He had trained hard, and he was ready for this amazing adventure. But then, a little problem popped up. Omar couldn't get the special paper he needed to enter the United States. This special paper is called a visa, and it's like a permission slip that lets you visit another country. Even though he was a special referee chosen by FIFA (we'll talk about them in a minute!), and even though he really, really wanted to go, he just couldn't get the permission. It must have been very disappointing for Omar. Imagine working so hard for something, being chosen for a super big event, and then not being able to go because of a problem with your travel papers. It's a tough situation, but sometimes these things happen, even to the best of us.

Who is FIFA?

Let's learn about FIFA now. FIFA stands for the 'Fédération Internationale de Football Association'. It's a very important group that manages football all over the world. Think of them as the big boss of global football. They organize the World Cup and make sure all the rules of football are the same everywhere. FIFA helps to grow football in all countries, including our African nations. They support players, teams, and referees like Omar. They make sure football is played fairly and that everyone gets a chance to be part of the beautiful game. They also hire the best referees to make sure the biggest matches, like those in the World Cup, are overseen by experienced and fair officials. **Did you know?** FIFA has more member countries than the United Nations! This shows how popular football is all around the globe. So, when Omar Artan was chosen, it was FIFA who selected him, because they know he is one of the best at his job. FIFA cares a lot about fairness and taking care of the people who work hard for football.

Good News for Omar!

Now for the really good news! Even though Omar could not travel to the United States and couldn't be a referee at the big World Cup games, FIFA decided to do something very kind and fair. They decided to still pay Omar his full fee! This means he will get all the money he was supposed to get, even if he couldn't go. Why did FIFA do this? Because it's the right thing to do! Omar was ready. He was prepared. He had been chosen. It wasn't his fault that he couldn't get the travel papers. FIFA understood that he had put in all the hard work and deserved to be paid for his time and effort, even if things didn't go as planned. This shows that FIFA is fair and supportive of its referees. It's like when you try your best at something, even if it doesn't work out perfectly, your teacher or parents still praise your effort. Omar is still a hero to us, and FIFA knows that. They wanted to make sure he wasn't sad or disappointed because of something he couldn't control.

What We Learn from Omar's Story

Omar Artan's story teaches us a few important things. First, sometimes things don't go exactly as we plan, even when we work very hard. But that doesn't mean our hard work is wasted. Second, it's very important to be fair and kind to others, just like FIFA was fair to Omar. Even though Omar couldn't go to the World Cup, he is still an amazing referee from Somalia. He is an inspiration to many young boys and girls in Africa who dream of being part of the world of football, whether as players, coaches, or even referees! His story reminds us that kindness and fairness always win in the end. **Did you know?** Africa has produced many incredible football referees who have officiated at World Cups and other big international tournaments, showing the great talent we have right here on our continent! So, let's give a big cheer for Omar Artan! He's a true champion of fairness and a wonderful example from our African home. Bravo, Omar!
summary: Somali referee Omar Artan, who was chosen for the World Cup but couldn't travel to the USA, will still receive his full payment from FIFA because they believe in being fair.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows that even if things don't go as planned, big organizations like FIFA can be fair and kind, making sure people who work hard are still taken care of.

originalTitle: Author Chimamanda Adichie accuses hospital of stalling review into son's death
title: A Famous Writer Asks Important Questions for Safety
content:
Hello, Friends!

Welcome to our special news from Africa! Today, we are going to talk about a very important lesson: asking questions to make things better and safer for everyone. Sometimes, sad things happen, but even then, asking questions helps us learn and grow.

Meet Chimamanda Adichie

There is a very smart and famous lady in Africa named Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie. She is from Nigeria, a big country in West Africa. Chimamanda is a wonderful writer. She writes amazing stories and books that people all over the world read. Her books teach us about life, about being brave, and about the special things that make us who we are. Many grown-ups love her stories, and some of her stories are even for young people like you! Did you know? Writers like Chimamanda use their imagination and words to create whole new worlds for us to explore when we read!

Her Little Son

Chimamanda has a family, just like many of us. She had a little son who was almost two years old. He was a very young boy, full of life and joy, just like many of you are now. Sadly, something very, very sad happened. Her little son became unwell and went to a hospital in a big city called Lagos, in Nigeria. While he was at the hospital, he passed away. This made Chimamanda and her family very, very sad. When someone we love passes away, it is a very difficult time. It's okay to feel sad and to cry. Grown-ups feel sad too.

Hospitals Help Us

Hospitals are very important places. They are where doctors and nurses work very hard to help people when they are sick or hurt. They give us medicine, they fix our broken bones, and they help us get better. Did you know? Doctors and nurses go to special schools for many, many years to learn how to help people stay healthy and get well. They are like real-life superheroes! We trust hospitals to take care of us and our families. They are meant to be safe places where we can get better.

Asking Why is Important

When something sad like this happens, especially in a place like a hospital, it's natural to have many questions. Chimamanda wants to understand what happened to her little son. She wants to know 'why'. She is asking for a special kind of meeting, like a big conversation, where people look at all the facts and try to understand everything that happened. This is sometimes called an 'inquest'. It's like when you try to figure out what happened to your toy when it suddenly breaks. You look at it, you think about what happened, and you ask questions. Chimamanda is doing this for a very important reason.

Why Do We Ask Questions?

Asking questions is very important. It helps us learn. When we learn what happened, we can sometimes find ways to stop it from happening again. Imagine if a playground swing was broken, and someone got hurt. If we didn't ask 'why' it broke, it might hurt someone else. But if we ask, we can fix the swing and make it safe for everyone. Chimamanda is asking questions so that hospitals can be even safer for all the other children and grown-ups who go there to get help. She wants to make sure that other families do not have to feel the same sadness. Did you know? Asking questions helps scientists discover new things, and it helps teachers explain things better. Asking questions is a superpower for learning!

Lagos, Nigeria: A Big Place!

The hospital where Chimamanda's son was is in a very big city called Lagos. Lagos is one of the biggest cities in all of Africa! It's a busy place with many people, cars, and markets. It's like a giant ant colony, always moving and buzzing with life. Many people live and work there. Did you know? Nigeria is a country in Africa with over 200 million people! That's a lot of friends! It has many different languages and cultures, making it a very colourful and exciting place.

Making Hospitals Even Better

Chimamanda believes that the hospital should help her find the answers. She feels that the hospital is making it hard for her to get the information she needs to understand what happened. This is why she is speaking out. She is being very brave. She is not just asking for herself, but for all the families in Nigeria and beyond. Her hope is that by asking these tough questions, hospitals will look at their rules and ways of working, and find ways to make them even better and safer for everyone. It's like when you play a game and you find a rule that doesn't work so well. If you talk about it, you can change the rule to make the game fairer and more fun for everyone. Chimamanda wants to make sure the 'rules' in hospitals are the very best they can be.

What Happens Next?

We hope that Chimamanda will get the answers she is looking for. We hope that everyone involved will work together to find out what happened and learn from it. When we are honest and look at everything carefully, we can always find ways to improve. This helps us build a better and safer world for all children and grown-ups.

Being Brave and Kind

Chimamanda is showing us that it's important to be brave and speak up when you think something needs to change. It's also important to remember that most doctors and nurses are kind people who try their very best every day. By asking questions and working together, we can make sure our hospitals continue to be places of healing and hope for everyone in Africa and around the world. Let's all hope for kindness and clear answers!
summary: Famous Nigerian writer Chimamanda Adichie is asking important questions about what happened to her little son in a Lagos hospital, hoping to find answers and help make hospitals safer for everyone.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us that asking questions, even when things are sad, can help make important places like hospitals better and safer for all children and grown-ups.

originalTitle: Kidnapped Nigerian retired general dies in captivity
title: A Sad Story About Being Safe in Our Big African Family
content:
Hello from Your Friend in Africa!

Hello, amazing kids! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share something important with you today. Sometimes, news can be a little bit sad, but even then, it's good to understand what's happening. Today, we're talking about a very special man from a country called Nigeria, which is a big and beautiful part of our African continent. We're going to talk about how everyone, even grown-ups, works hard to make sure our communities and countries are safe places for all children to grow up, play, and learn. It's a team effort!

Who Was Major General Rabe?

Once upon a time, there was a very important man in Nigeria named Major General Rabe Abubakar. Now, a "Major General" is like a super big boss in the army. Imagine a leader who helps train many soldiers to keep everyone safe and sound. That's what a general does! They are like superheroes who protect their country. Major General Rabe used to work very, very hard for many years, helping to keep Nigeria safe. He was a brave and respected man. After many years of important work, he became "retired." This means he stopped working every day, but he was still a very wise and important person in his community. Did you know? An army is a group of brave people, called soldiers, who wear special uniforms and work together to protect their country from bad people and help in emergencies. They are like a big safety team for the whole country!

What Happened to Major General Rabe?

Sadly, something very worrying happened to Major General Rabe. Some people who cause trouble, people who don't follow rules and make others scared, took him away from his home. This is sometimes called being "kidnapped." It means someone is taken away when they don't want to go, and it's a very sad and wrong thing to do. When he was taken, many people in Nigeria and all over Africa felt very sad and worried for him and his family. Everyone hoped he would come back home safely very soon. But, even though many people wished and hoped, Major General Rabe sadly passed away while he was being held by these trouble-making people, far away from his family and friends. This made many people's hearts feel heavy. It's important to remember that when bad things like this happen, it reminds us how important it is for everyone to be safe and free. Nobody should ever be taken away from their home or their family without wanting to go.

Why Do Bad Things Sometimes Happen?

In parts of our big, beautiful Africa, just like in other parts of the world, sometimes there are people who cause problems. We call these "safety challenges" or "security challenges." This means there are times and places where people might not feel completely safe because of others who don't follow the rules or are unkind. These people might cause trouble for different reasons. Sometimes they are confused, or they want things that don't belong to them, or they just make bad choices. But no matter the reason, it's never okay to scare people, hurt people, or take them away from their homes. Did you know? Nigeria is a country in West Africa and is home to over 200 million people, making it the most populated country in Africa! Just like a big family, sometimes even in the biggest families, there can be disagreements or problems.

What Are People Doing to Help?

Now, here's the super important part: even though sad things happen, there are SO many good people working hard every single day to make things better! Police officers, soldiers (just like Major General Rabe used to be), community leaders, and even everyday grown-ups are all working together. Their job is to make sure everyone is safe, to stop bad people from causing trouble, and to help those who are in danger. They work like a big, united team, looking out for each other and trying their best to bring peace and safety to every home and every village. They investigate when bad things happen, they try to help people who are in trouble, and they teach everyone about being safe. It's a huge job, but many brave and kind people are doing it with all their hearts. Did you know? In many African countries, communities have special groups called 'Neighbourhood Watch' or 'Community Policing' where everyday people help the police keep an eye on things and make their local areas safer. It shows how we can all work together!

Learning from Sad News

This sad story about Major General Rabe reminds us of something very, very important: safety. It shows us that we must keep working hard to make sure everyone in our big African family feels safe, no matter where they live. When important people like Major General Rabe pass away in such a sad way, it highlights that there are still problems that need fixing. It makes all the grown-ups who lead our countries think even harder about how to protect everyone, especially children. It encourages them to work faster and smarter to solve these "safety challenges" so that peace can grow everywhere. It's like when you see a broken toy; you learn that you need to be more careful with it next time, or you need to fix it. This sad news is like a reminder that we must fix the safety problems.

Hope for a Brighter Tomorrow

Even when we hear sad news, we must always remember that there is so much good in the world, and there is always hope! Many, many good people are dedicated to making Africa a safer and happier place for you, the children, to grow up. They dream of a future where all children can play freely outside, go to school without worry, and live happily with their families. And guess what? You, by being kind, listening to your grown-ups, and helping your friends, are also a part of making our world a better and safer place. Let's all remember Major General Rabe and work together, big and small, for a future filled with peace, safety, and happiness for everyone in our amazing African continent. Goodbye for now, my wonderful friends!
summary: Major General Rabe, a respected retired army leader in Nigeria, sadly passed away after being taken away by trouble-making people, reminding everyone that working together for safety is super important across Africa.
whyMatters: This sad story reminds us that even important grown-ups face dangers, and it shows why we all, especially leaders and communities, must work extra hard to make sure everyone is safe and happy in Africa.

originalTitle: Real Madrid agree £51.8m deal for Chelsea's Cucurella
title: Wow! A Big Football Star is Moving Teams!
content:
Hello Football Friends!

Hello, my super awesome football friends across Africa! Your friend, the kid-friendly news reporter, is here with some exciting news from the big world of football! Get ready to cheer because something really cool is happening with some of the most famous football teams in the whole wide world! We're talking about players moving, like when you move schools, but for really, really big teams!

Who Are Real Madrid and Chelsea?

First, let's talk about two famous football clubs. Think of a football club like your favourite school team, but much, much bigger! One team is called **Real Madrid**. They are a super famous team from a country called Spain. They often wear beautiful white jerseys and have won so many big trophies, it's amazing! They are like champions of champions! **Did you know?** Real Madrid has won the biggest club competition in Europe, called the Champions League, more times than any other team! They are true legends! The other team is **Chelsea**. They are also super famous and strong! Chelsea is from England and they usually wear blue colours. They have also won many big games and have a lot of amazing players. Imagine two giant, super-strong teams playing against each other – that's Real Madrid and Chelsea!

Meet Marc Cucurella!

Now, let's meet our football star! His name is **Marc Cucurella**. He is a very talented football player. Before this news, Marc played for Chelsea, the team in blue. Marc plays a special role on the field, he is a **defender**. What does a defender do? Well, imagine you are playing a game of catch, and someone is trying to take your ball away. A defender is like a super guard who stops the other team from scoring goals! They are very important because they protect their team's goal. Marc is very good at running fast, tackling the ball (taking it nicely from the other team), and helping his team keep the ball.

What is a 'Deal' and Big Money?

So, what's the big news? Real Madrid wants Marc Cucurella to play for *their* team! When one big football team wants a player from another big football team, they have to make a 'deal'. A 'deal' means they talk and agree on a price. It's like if you have a special toy, and your friend wants it, they might offer you something in return, right? But with big football teams, it's about a lot of money! Real Madrid has agreed to pay a very, very big amount of money for Marc. It's about **£51.8 million**! That's a super huge number! Can you imagine how many sweets or how many footballs that is? It's like having millions and millions of little coins! That's how much they believe Marc will help their team become even stronger and win more games. It shows how much they value him as a player. **Did you know?** Football is the most popular sport in the whole wide world! Billions of people love to watch and play it!

Why Do Teams Buy Players?

That's a great question! Why do big teams spend so much money to get new players? Well, it's like adding a new, super-fast runner or a super-strong defender to your school team. You do it to make your team better! Real Madrid wants to make their team super, super strong so they can win even more big trophies and make their fans (people who love the team) very happy. Marc Cucurella is a fantastic player who can help them do just that! He will bring new skills and a lot of energy to Real Madrid.

What is the World Cup?

There's one more important part of the news: Marc Cucurella will join Real Madrid **after the World Cup**. What is the World Cup? It's the biggest, most exciting football party on Earth! Every four years, countries from all over the world send their best players to play against each other to see who is the champion of the world! It's like a super big tournament where everyone watches. Many of our favourite African teams and players also play in the World Cup, making us all proud! So, Marc will be busy either playing in or getting ready for this huge tournament. After the World Cup is over, he will then officially join his new team, Real Madrid. It's a very exciting time for him! **Did you know?** The World Cup happens only once every four years, which makes it very special and exciting when it does happen!

What Happens Next?

Soon, Marc Cucurella will put on the famous white jersey of Real Madrid. He will train with his new teammates, learn new strategies, and get ready to play in big matches. We will see him running on the field, defending his new goal, and helping Real Madrid try to win even more trophies! It's a new adventure for Marc, and for Real Madrid, it means a new super player is joining their family.

Dream Big, Little Stars!

Isn't football wonderful? It brings people together, makes us cheer, and shows us what hard work can do. Many amazing football stars from Africa play in big teams all over the world, just like Marc will play for Real Madrid. Keep playing football, keep dreaming big, and who knows? Maybe one day, we'll be reading news about *you* moving to a big, famous team! Keep shining, my little football champions! That's all for today's exciting news. Back to you soon!
summary: Real Madrid, a super famous football team from Spain, has agreed to buy a very good defender named Marc Cucurella from another big team, Chelsea, for a lot of money, and he will join them after the big World Cup football tournament.
whyMatters: This is important because when a famous player like Marc moves to a big team like Real Madrid, it makes both teams change and gets everyone excited to see new games and who will win the next big trophy!

originalTitle: The 20-year-old who stole the show on Scotland's World Cup return

content: Ben Gannon-Doak wasn't born when Scotland last played at the men's World Cup. But the 20-year-old was at the heart of their long-awaited return and their first win at the tournament in 36 years as he stole the show against Haiti.
